1: shadow Clap your hands, all peoples! Shout to God with loud shouts of joy! The universal acclamation directed at Christ's enthronement: at the name of Jesus every knee should bow, in heaven and on earth and under the earth, and every tongue confess that Jesus Christ is Lord (Philippians 2:10-11). The clapping of all peoples is the eschatological praise the Lamb receives (Revelation 5:12).
2: shadow For the LORD, the Most High, is to be feared, a great king over all the earth. Christ is declared the great king over all the earth: All authority in heaven and on earth has been given to me (Matthew 28:18). The title Son of the Most High God is used of Jesus by the demon-possessed man (Mark 5:7), who recognizes his absolute sovereignty.
3: shadow He subdued peoples under us, and nations under our feet. The subjection of all things under Christ's feet: he must reign until he has put all his enemies under his feet (1 Corinthians 15:25). The church shares in this subduing (Romans 16:20 — the God of peace will soon crush Satan under your feet) as co-heirs with the risen King.
4: shadow He chose our heritage for us, the pride of Jacob whom he loves. God's election of a people — fulfilled in Christ who is the elect one (Isaiah 42:1; 1 Peter 2:4) and in whom the elect are chosen: he chose us in him before the foundation of the world (Ephesians 1:4). The heritage becomes the new creation; the pride of Jacob becomes the Israel of God (Galatians 6:16).
5: direct God has gone up with a shout, the LORD with the sound of a trumpet. The Ascension of Christ: he was lifted up, and a cloud took him out of their sight (Acts 1:9), accompanied by angels. Paul applies the ascending motif in Ephesians 4:8-10: When he ascended on high he led a host of captives and gave gifts to men. God goes up — the risen Christ ascends to enthronement at the Father's right hand.
6: shadow Sing praises to God, sing praises! Sing praises to our King, sing praises! The fourfold call to praise is the church's liturgical response to the Ascension. Revelation's throne-room scenes (Rev 4-5) show the living creatures and elders singing praises to the one who sits on the throne and to the Lamb — the singular praise of God and Christ.
7: shadow For God is the King of all the earth; sing praises with a psalm! Christ as King of all the earth: the kingdom of the world has become the kingdom of our Lord and of his Christ, and he shall reign forever and ever (Revelation 11:15). The singing with a psalm is what the church does — gathered around Christ as the center of every psalm.
8: shadow God reigns over the nations; God sits on his holy throne. The enthronement of Christ: when he had made purification for sins, he sat down at the right hand of the Majesty on high (Hebrews 1:3). His session at the right hand is his reign over the nations — the Psalm's vision of God on his holy throne is fulfilled in the ascended Son who shares the divine throne (Revelation 3:21).
9: shadow The princes of the peoples gather as the people of the God of Abraham. For the shields of the earth belong to God; he is highly exalted! The gathering of the nations under the God of Abraham — fulfilled in the Gentile mission of the church. He is highly exalted — the language of Philippians 2:9: God has highly exalted him and bestowed on him the name that is above every name. The shields of the earth belong to the one exalted above all.
